name: The Go Engineer v3 — Cross References

architectural_decision.decision: Break the locked v2 curriculum and rebuild around the Report 1 phase-based zero-to-software-engineer path.
architectural_decision.v2_policy: curriculum.v2.json is frozen legacy import data, not the active source of truth.
architectural_decision.core_rule: Keep good lessons, break the old path, optimize for learner progression, proof, documentation quality, and job readiness.
architectural_decision.full_bundle_policy: Split files are authoritative. curriculum.v3.full.generated.json is an optional generated snapshot for review/export only.

style_rules.preview_only: Preview only: Explained fully in Module {num} — {lesson title}

semantic_quality_requirements.rule: Every related link must have a non-generic reason. Generic reasons like 'Related concepts within the same module' or 'This project reinforces concepts from this lesson' are not acceptable in golden-status items.
acceptable_reason_types: operational dependency: {A} must be understood to debug {B}
shared failure mode: both involve {failure category}
architectural connection: {A} and {B} are ports/adapters in the same boundary
production failure overlap: both contribute to {failure scenario}
semantic_quality_requirements.enforcement: validate-crossrefs must flag generic reasons on items with zero_magic_status=golden
